reviews.review_title: Nearly a solid Ace Combat clone
reviews.review_description:
Looking past the terrible voice acting and meaningless dialogue trying too hard to be funny, the game is enjoyable for me. Controls are smooth, dogfighting can be thrilling, and there's a nice selection of planes.
 
But I have some serious issues. 
For starters, machine guns don't even do damage. 
They're just there for looks and sounds I suppose.
Second, no flares. Have fun dipping and dodging wildly from missiles you can't see until it's too late.
Finally, there are enemy AI and models for fighters such as the F-22, F-117, and SU-47 that aren't accessible to the player. This confuses me. If you have the models, variables, damage and flight speed in the game, why limit those aircraft to strictly the AI enemies?

I still enjoy playing often, regardless of the flaws. It gives off a genuine Ace Combat feeling, the closest I can get with a Quest. Awesome work by the devs :)
But you should add the gun for the a10

reviews.review_title: Not bad, but can definitely be better
reviews.review_description:
So I just happened to get this game and I can't argue about the price. And overall the game isn't terrible but there are some fine points that would make this amazing.

1. Flight controls while in cockpit mode hands don't stick on controls, actual movements to control the jet are way too small, they should be a bit more relative to holding a flight stick. 

2. Cockpits are gorgeous however in first person mode the HUD overlay isn't scaled properly in cockpit mode, way too large, ruins a bit of the immersion. Makes it a little difficult to fly.

3. Dialogue in the campaign, a little cheesy but I can live with it.

4. Flight physics models can be given a little TLC I see where the game is going, it definitely has an ACE Combat feel to it, but it needs a flight physics model that is a little smoother in relation to pilot control input.

All that said, with a little more polish id actually pay for this game. Definitely a good starting point to build from.

Air Brigade 2 is an exhilarating VR arcade combat game that delivers on all fronts. The flying mechanics are responsive and intuitive, making dogfights a thrilling and intense experience. The game feels like a modern Ace Combat in VR, offering fast-paced action that's a blast to play. Visually, the game is impressive, with detailed cockpits and aircraft models that enhance the feeling of being a fighter pilot. While the campaign may be on the shorter side, the multiplayer combat and the satisfying progression of unlocking new, more powerful jets provide plenty of reasons to keep coming back for more. It's a fantastic game for anyone looking for a fun and immersive flight combat experience on their VR headset.

PixelPrisonVreviws

reviews.review_title: Honestly one of my favorite vr games
reviews.review_description: I really enjoy this game and I’ve always wanted to play something like it just never found one. The only thing is that I was really looking forwards to first person but I can’t seem to adjust the view at all in first person so that the control panel is out of the way enough for me to see in front of me. It sort of covers up my entire view of what is in front of my plane, so I’ve really only played the game in third person. Other than that, I have a great time and enjoy the mechanics of the game.
